beats.narration: One flaw, three pictures. Every ultrasonic display comes from the same chain. What changes is how it's drawn — and which question it answers.

payload.title: ONE FLAW, 3 VIEWS
payload.sub: Every display answers a different question.

beats.narration: Same flaw, three ways. A-scan: the waveform at one spot. Stacked along a line: the B-scan slice. One gated value per point: the C-scan.

payload.heading: Same flaw, three ways to draw it

beats.narration: Each has a blind spot. A-scan: one spot, no map. B-scan: the first reflector shadows what's beneath. C-scan: only what the gate caught.

payload.heading: Each view has a blind spot

chips.t: C-scan — only what the gate caught

beats.narration: From the probe, computerized systems digitize each waveform, stamp it with encoder position, gate it in software, and store everything for re-analysis.

payload.heading: Data flow: probe to archive
payload.note: STORED WAVEFORMS CAN BE RE-GATED OFFLINE

beats.narration: Pick by question, not habit. This echo? A-scan. Depth along a line? B-scan. Area covered? C-scan. Final call: back to the waveform.

payload.sub: The final call comes back to the waveform.
